trivia_bullets.text: The card name is a portmanteau pun on 'jack-in-the-box' and 'attack.' The flavor text 'Pop goes the evil.' directly parodies the Pop Goes the Weasel nursery rhyme that accompanies most jack-in-the-box toys.
sources: CoolStuffInc: The Flavor Gems of Duskmourn: House of Horrors, Jay Annelli, 2024

trivia_bullets.text: Colorless {3} Artifact Creature -- Toy; base 2/4. Attack trigger optionally pumps to 6/4 but auto-sacrifices at the NEXT end step if the buff is taken -- not at end of turn, meaning the creature survives through the opponent's cleanup phase. EDHREC rank: 18,088.

Attack-in-the-Box is the first tournament-legal jack-in-the-box creature in Magic. The closest prior card is Jack-in-the-Mox (Unglued / Unsanctioned, silver-border), a comedic {0} artifact with a random die-roll effect including a 1-in-6 chance to lose 5 life and sacrifice itself -- the same horror-toy premise but entirely silver-border and tournament-illegal.
sources: Scryfall: Jack-in-the-Mox, UND no. 78; Scryfall: reprint field on DSK/242 = false

trivia_bullets.text: Artist Domenico Cava also illustrated Patched Plaything (DSK no. 24); both DSK Toy Artifact Creatures in this inventory share an artist. No Hipsters of the Coast coverage found for Cava at time of research.
sources: Scryfall: artist fields on DSK/242 and DSK/24

related_cards.name: Patched Plaything
related_cards.note: Same artist, same Toy subtype, same batch

related_cards.name: Jack-in-the-Mox
related_cards.note: Prior silver-border jack-in-the-box card; thematic predecessor
